data.title: Asia markets mixed as tech rally cools following Fed hold and mixed earnings
data.description:
Asian stock markets slowed down on Thursday. Technology companies saw their prices drop after a strong run, as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve’s decision to keep interest rates the same and a mix of good and bad financial results from major U.S. tech firms.

Gold extended its record-breaking rally above $5,500 per ounce on Thursday, hitting a fresh high amid continued weakness in the US dollar and heightened economic and geopolitical uncertainty.

Singapore’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate held steady at 2% in Q4 2025, unchanged from the previous quarter, preliminary estimates showed. Singapore’s Domestic Supply Price Index dropped by 3.3% year-on-year in December 2025, reversing an upwardly revised 2.8% increase in the previous month.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.14% to around 53,500 on Thursday, marking its third consecutive session of gains. The Japanese yen was around 153.2 per dollar on Thursday, following a nearly 1% decline in the previous session.

Traders remain cautious about the risk of unilateral action from Tokyo, although Bank of Japan data shows that authorities have not officially intervened in the market so far. Political uncertainty also weighed on the yen in the run-up to the Feb. 8 lower house snap election.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.02% to around 4,140 while the Shenzhen Component edged up 0.2% to 14,370 on Thursday.

In other news, Goldman Sachs projects that Chinese equities could attract roughly $518 billion in new domestic capital this year, driven by strong corporate earnings and improving market sentiment.

Caution also set in ahead of China’s official January PMI release this weekend, following late-2025 gains in both manufacturing and services.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.50% to 27,789 in Thursday morning trade.

The Hong Kong Monetary Authority kept its base rate unchanged at 4.0% on January 29, 2026, following the U.S. Federal Reserve’s decision hours earlier to hold its target range at 3%–3.75%.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.52% to 81,752 in morning trade on Thursday, erasing gains from the previous two sessions as traders grew cautious ahead of the Union Budget 2026 due this weekend. 

However, a trade agreement between India and the EU capped the fall. Most sectors traded in the red, including auto, tech, banking, and financial services. India will immediately slash duties on high-end European cars to 30% from as high as 110% under its new trade deal with the EU, an official said, opening the tightly controlled market to luxury carmakers such as BMW and Mercedes-Benz, as Reuters reported.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.12% to around 8,890 in Thursday morning trade, extending losses from the previous session. The Australian dollar rose above $0.70 on Thursday, hitting a fresh three-year high, driven by surging gold prices and growing expectations of an imminent rate hike.

Australia’s export prices increased by 3.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 2025, recovering from a 0.9% decline in Q3. Australia’s import prices increased 0.9% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 2025, defying market expectations of a 0.2% decline and reversing a 0.4% fall in Q3.

The Philippines’ GDP grew 3% year-on-year in Q4 2025, below expectations of 3.8% and slowing from 3.9% in the previous quarter.

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended flat after the Federal Reserve held its rates unchanged, as largely expected, ahead of earnings results from major AI players after the closing bell.

U.S. stock futures fell slightly on Thursday as investors digested earnings from megacap tech firms alongside a widely expected decision by the Fed to hold rates steady.: Dow -0.06%; S&P 500 +0.15%; Nasdaq +0.33%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Norway’s $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund reduces stakes in top U.S. tech firms
data.description:
• Norway’s $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund reduced its holdings in major U.S. tech companies, including its top position in Nvidia (NVDA), during the second half of 2025, according to newly released portfolio data.
  • By year-end, Norges Bank Investment Management, which oversees the fund, had trimmed all of its top four tech holdings, which also included Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL).
  • The fund cut its stake in Nvidia to 1.26% from 1.32% earlier, while Microsoft's holdings were slashed to 1.26% from 1.35% earlier. The two companies still feature among the fund's most valued investments, with Apple (AAPL) retaining the second spot.
  • Overall, U.S. investments accounted for 52.9% of the fund’s assets at the end of 2025 across equities, bonds, and property, up from 52.4% six months earlier, the data showed.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Updating the goods portion of the advance report and offering initial data on services, this report provides complete information on cross-border trade.

Imports and the deficit are expected to snap back to show a deficit of $45 billion after tariff effects slashed imports, leading to a modest $29.4 billion deficit in October.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims are expected to be up to 205K from 200K, as observers see 200K as too low to represent current conditions.

8:30 AM Productivity and Costs

Productivity measures the growth of labor efficiency in producing the economy's goods and services.

Q3 is expected to be unrevised at 4.9 percent productivity growth with ULC unrevised at minus 1.9 percent.

10:00 AM Factory Orders

Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods.

Orders are expected to reverse the 1.3 percent drop of October with a 1.3 percent increase in November.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report 

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country.

1:00 PM 7-Yr Note Auction

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $44 billion of 7-year notes.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Home Depot chops 800 corporate jobs, orders staff back to office full-time
Singapore's GIC partners with Sony Music to acquire music catalog assets
data.description:
• Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund GIC has teamed up with Sony Music Group (SONY) to acquire music catalog assets, the companies said Thursday.
  • The statement said the partnership will combine the operational capabilities of Sony Music Group, the music arm of Japanese ‌conglomerate Sony Group (SONY), with GIC's long-term ​capital to manage potential assets.
  • Bloomberg News reported that the vehicle could include $2 billion ‍to $3 billion in investment. GIC and Sony did not provide an amount in the statement.
  • "The music ecosystem is a resilient sector with attractive long-term growth prospects and we are excited about the next stage of streaming monetization through premiumization and subscriber growth in emerging markets," Girish Karira, Head of Integrated Strategies Group, GIC said.

data.title: IBM expects 10% software growth and $15.7B free cash flow in 2026 while advancing AI and hybrid cloud
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) (IBM) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Arvind Krishna, CEO, President & Chairman, highlighted that "we are excited about the progress we made in 2025, delivering 6% revenue growth, our highest level of revenue growth in many years and $14.7 billion of free cash flow, our highest level of cash generation in over a decade." He stated that IBM is executing its strategy to advance as a "software-led, hybrid cloud and AI platform company," with software now representing approximately 45% of IBM's business. Software revenue grew 9% for the year, marking the highest annual growth rate in history, with three out of four subsegments delivering double-digit growth rates. Infrastructure revenue was up 10%, driven by record performance in IBM Z, and consulting returned to growth in the second half, supported by strong GenAI momentum.

  • Krishna described the successful launch and strength of the z17 mainframe, noting it processed "50% more AI inferencing operations per day than z16" and achieved the highest annual revenue for IBM Z in about 20 years. He also announced the acquisition of Confluent, stating it will unify IBM's hybrid cloud and automation solutions.

  • On productivity, Krishna said, "we are well ahead of that, exiting 2025 with $4.5 billion of annual run rate savings." He also referenced the Project Bob AI-based software development system, with over 20,000 IBM-ers using it and reporting productivity gains averaging 45%.

  • James Kavanaugh, CFO & Senior VP, stated: "We exited 2025 beating all of these metrics, delivering 6% revenue growth, 100 basis points of operating pretax margin expansion, 17% adjusted EBITDA growth and $14.7 billion of free cash flow, growing 16% over last year. This represents our highest free cash flow margin in reported history."

Outlook

  • For 2026, management expects to "sustain constant currency revenue growth of 5% plus in 2026 and free cash flow to be up about $1 billion year-over-year, growing high single digits" (Kavanaugh). Software revenue is projected to grow 10%, led by organic growth, recurring revenue, GenAI traction, M&A synergies, and monetization of IBM Z placements.

  • Consulting revenue is anticipated to accelerate to low to mid-single digits for the year, with backlog penetration over 25% supporting the outlook. Infrastructure revenue is expected to be down low single digits, with Z growth in the first quarter balanced by product cycle dynamics.

  • Kavanaugh detailed that "we anticipate absorbing about $600 million of dilution from Confluent in 2026," but expects Confluent to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA within the first full year and to free cash flow in year 2 post close. IBM aims for an incremental $1 billion in productivity savings in 2026, reaching $5.5 billion annual run rate savings by year-end.

Financial Results

  • In Q4, IBM delivered total revenue growth of 9%. Software revenue growth accelerated to 11%, with Data up 19% and Automation up 14%. Consulting was up 1%, and Infrastructure rose 17%, with Hybrid Infrastructure up 24% and IBM Z up 61% year-over-year for the quarter.

  • Annual recurring revenue (ARR) was $23.6 billion, up over $2 billion from the end of 2024. Segment profit margins expanded by 100 basis points in Software, 180 basis points in Consulting, and 450 basis points in Infrastructure. IBM ended 2025 with $14.5 billion in cash, invested $8.3 billion in acquisitions, and returned $6.3 billion to shareholders via dividends.

Q&A

  • Brent Thill, Jefferies: Asked about components of accelerating software growth and organic-led initiatives. Krishna explained that Automation is on a "secular demand increase," Data is benefiting from AI products, and Red Hat's growth is projected in the mid-single digits, with OpenShift at "almost $2 billion and running at a 30% growth rate."

  • Amit Daryanani, Evercore: Asked about free cash flow outperformance and guidance for $15.7 billion in 2026. Kavanaugh answered, "We entered the year...we said $13.5 billion, and...we posted $14.7 billion...highest free cash flow we've seen in well over a decade," attributing growth to revenue acceleration and operating leverage.

  • Benjamin Reitzes, Melius: Asked about Red Hat's growth trajectory. Kavanaugh noted Red Hat's guidance is "prudent...because we only need that at double digit to get Software over the line of double digit. Upside, we'll deliver upside to Software overall."

  • Wamsi Mohan, BofA: Inquired about memory pricing affecting server cycles and PTI improvement cadence. Krishna said memory pressures will persist, but "I don't expect that the overall server dynamic...is actually going to be any headwind to us on the Hybrid Cloud or the Linux side."

  • James Schneider, Goldman Sachs: Asked about Consulting growth. Kavanaugh responded, "We're encouraged by the inflection shift...back to durable, sustainable growth," with backlog at $32 billion and GenAI accounting for over 25% of backlog.

  • Erik Woodring, Morgan Stanley: Asked about sustainability in Z infrastructure. Krishna explained increasing adoption due to sovereignty, developer tools, and in-line AI capabilities, with ongoing client interest in installing GenAI cards.

  • Matthew Swanson, RBC: Asked about GenAI’s role in enterprise transformation. Krishna projected that "50% of the enterprise usage of AI is going to be in either a private cloud or...their own data centers. And the other 50% is going to be usage of public models."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability and acceleration of growth, with a generally positive but probing tone around cash flow, Red Hat, and AI monetization. Questions were direct but not confrontational.

  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, especially in prepared remarks, with frequent references to historic performance and future confidence, using phrases such as "we feel confident about that $15.7 billion, and our job is to beat it this year" (Kavanaugh).

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analyst and management tone shifted from cautious optimism to stronger confidence, with more emphasis on durable growth and momentum.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more explicit, with targets of 5%+ revenue growth, 10% software growth, and $15.7 billion free cash flow, compared to prior quarter guidance of "more than 5%" revenue growth and about $14 billion free cash flow for 2025.

  • The software segment's growth outlook has moved from "approaching double digits" to an explicit 10% for 2026. Red Hat guidance is now more conservative, reflecting recent booking dynamics and the impact of the U.S. government shutdown.

  • Analyst questions remain focused on sustainability of growth, AI monetization, and M&A integration, but current quarter reveals increased focus on productivity initiatives and Confluent acquisition impacts.

  • Management confidence is stronger, highlighting historic profitability, free cash flow, and software mix, while analysts probe for details on segment drivers and headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Krishna flagged continued volatility in server memory pricing but stated it is not expected to impede Hybrid Cloud or Linux growth.

  • Kavanaugh acknowledged $600 million dilution from Confluent in 2026, mitigated by productivity initiatives and revenue synergies.

  • Analyst concerns were noted around Red Hat's bookings deceleration and potential infrastructure cycle headwinds, but management addressed these as manageable within current guidance.

Final Takeaway

IBM’s management projects sustained momentum into 2026, anchored by a clear shift to software-led growth, robust free cash flow guidance, and accelerating AI and hybrid cloud adoption. Productivity initiatives and recent acquisitions, including Confluent, are positioned as key drivers for continued margin expansion and operating leverage, supporting confidence in meeting or exceeding the newly outlined growth targets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lam Research outlines $135B WFE forecast for 2026 as AI-driven demand accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Timothy Archer stated that Lam Research ended calendar year 2025 with revenue ahead of the midpoint of guidance, while gross margins, operating margins, and EPS all exceeded the high end of the range. Archer emphasized, “Our performance demonstrates continued strong execution in an accelerating semiconductor demand environment.”
  • Archer highlighted the company’s progress toward its vision to more than double revenue and profit over five years, saying, “The vision we shared was to more than double Lam's revenue and profit over the next 5 years. Today, we are well on our way.”
  • Archer detailed the company’s leadership in deposition and etch capabilities vital to AI-related technology transitions, including gate-all-around transistors, backside power deposition, and 3D advanced packaging. He noted, “We achieved record revenues of more than $20 billion and expanded our served available market, or SAM, share of WFE into the mid-30s percent range.”
  • Archer announced that the Akara etch system doubled its installed base over the past year, with significant wins in advanced DRAM and foundry/logic. Akara’s adoption is expected to grow by roughly 2x in next-generation gate-all-around devices.
  • Archer reported that the Customer Support Business Group’s installed base topped 100,000 chambers, with revenue growing faster than the increase in installed base units.
  • CFO Douglas Bettinger stated, “In calendar year 2025, revenue was a record coming in at $20.6 billion, which was up 27% year-over-year.” Bettinger also noted, “Gross margin was 49.9%, the highest result as a combined company for the full year since the Novellus merger back in 2012.”

Outlook

  • Bettinger provided guidance for the March 2026 quarter: “We're expecting revenue of $5.7 billion, plus or minus $300 million. We're expecting gross margin of 49%, plus or minus 1 percentage point... We're forecasting earnings per share of $1.35, plus or minus $0.10, based on a share count of approximately 1.26 billion shares.”
  • Archer indicated that WFE for 2026 is initially viewed at $135 billion, with growth weighted to the second half of the year and robust investment expected across DRAM and leading-edge foundry/logic.
  • Management reiterated that 2026 is expected to be a second half weighted year due to clean room space constraints, with China revenue projected to remain flattish year-over-year.

Financial Results

  • December quarter revenue was reported at $5.34 billion, marking the tenth consecutive quarter of revenue growth.
  • Deferred revenue at quarter end was $2.25 billion, down sequentially due to a reduction in customer advanced down payments.
  • Foundry represented 59% of systems revenue, memory 34% (with record DRAM revenue at 23%), and nonvolatile memory 11%. China accounted for 35% of total revenue.
  • Customer Support Business Group revenue for the quarter was approximately $2 billion, up 12% sequentially.
  • December quarter gross margin was 49.7%, operating margin was 34.3%, and diluted EPS was $1.27. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $6.2 billion, with inventory turns at 2.7x.
  • Capital expenditures were $261 million for the quarter, driven by increased investment in manufacturing and R&D capacity.

Q&A

  • Timothy Arcuri, UBS: Asked about WFE constraints and the magnitude of impact. Bettinger responded, “It's hard for us to put a number on it... plans are somewhat fluid... people are trying to figure out how to get a little more clean room space.”
  • Arcuri probed gross margin guidance and China mix. Bettinger replied, “Yes, it's customer mix, it's going to be less rich in the March quarter... The mix component of both products as well as customer is an important item.”
  • Christopher Muse, Cantor Fitzgerald: Inquired about CSBG growth sustainability. Archer explained that CSBG growth is being driven by equipment intelligence for predictive maintenance and Dextro cobots, with continued top line and margin improvement expected.
  • Atif Malik, Citi: Asked about DRAM timing for 4F [Square] and Akara’s SAM share. Archer stated, “4F [Square] is probably on the back end... after this next big wave of fab openings.”
  • Vivek Arya, BofA Securities: Asked about share gains and China’s WFE contribution. Archer said, “We plan to increase our share of WFE again this year,” and expects China to remain flattish, becoming a smaller portion of revenue as technology-driven segments grow.
  • Multiple analysts questioned the sustainability and timing of NAND and DRAM upgrades versus capacity additions. Bettinger and Archer indicated upgrades are prioritized until new clean room capacity is available, with greenfield additions expected in future years.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was generally positive but persistent in probing for specifics on WFE constraints, gross margin impacts, supply chain dynamics, and the timing of technology transitions, reflecting cautious optimism but also a need for clarity on how supply and demand dynamics will play out.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing strong execution and outlook. During Q&A, management occasionally deflected on precise figures, using phrases such as “it's hard for us to put a number on it” and “we're not going to put a number on it,” but reiterated confidence in continued outperformance and share gains.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident but with heightened urgency around scaling and meeting accelerated AI-driven demand, while analysts maintained a similar level of probing.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a notable increase in guidance for both revenue and EPS for March 2026 compared to the prior quarter’s guidance, as well as higher actual December quarter results versus previous guidance.
  • Share gains were explicitly targeted again, with Akara’s adoption and advanced packaging growth highlighted as outperforming prior projections.
  • Foundry’s share of systems revenue remained strong, while DRAM revenue set a new record and China’s contribution decreased from the prior quarter.
  • The management’s language signaled even more urgency and focus on scaling operations and supply chain resilience compared to last quarter’s discussions.
  • Analysts’ focus remained on technology transitions, capacity constraints, and the timing of new investments, echoing previous concerns but with increased attention on how Lam will navigate industry-wide bottlenecks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Clean room space limitations were repeatedly cited as a major constraint to both Lam’s and industry-wide growth, with management acknowledging that “plans are somewhat fluid” and that demand is outpacing available capacity.
  • Customer mix shifts, especially with China, are expected to pressure gross margin in the near term.
  • Supply chain and inventory management challenges were discussed, but management reported no significant bottlenecks as of now, attributing improvements to post-pandemic investments in operational resilience.
  • Analyst questions highlighted uncertainty regarding the timing and magnitude of NAND and DRAM upgrades versus new capacity additions, and the potential impact of shifting customer priorities.

Final Takeaway

Lam Research highlighted record financial performance in 2025 and outlined a strong outlook for 2026, forecasting WFE to reach $135 billion and positioning the company to gain further market share through accelerated technology transitions in AI, DRAM, and advanced packaging. Management emphasized robust growth in both core product areas and customer support, while noting persistent industry constraints around clean room space and evolving customer mix. The company’s focus on scaling operational capacity and advancing its product roadmap remains central to meeting heightened semiconductor demand in the AI era.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Las Vegas Sands signals focus on margin recovery in Macao and continued growth at Marina Bay Sands through 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Robert Goldstein, Chairman and CEO, highlighted record results at Marina Bay Sands, stating, "Marina Bay Sands has delivered EBITDA of $806 million, simply the greatest quarter in the history of casino hotels. We exceeded $2.9 billion of EBITDA this year. Mass gaming and slot win exceeded $951 million this quarter, which is up 118% in Q4 in 2019, up 27% from Q4 last year." Goldstein acknowledged disappointment with Macao's $608 million of EBITDA for the quarter, but noted mass market revenue share gains. He emphasized, "The team is in the right place, and we will deliver better results in 2026."
  • Patrick Dumont, President and COO, reported, "If we had held as expected in our rolling program, our EBITDA would have been lower by $26 million. When adjusted for higher-than-expected hold of the rolling segment, our EBITDA margin for the Macao portfolio of properties would have been 28.9%, down 390 basis points compared to the fourth quarter of 2024." Dumont also announced, "We repurchased $500 million of LVS stock during the quarter. We also paid our recurring quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share. During the fourth quarter, we purchased $66 million of SCL stock, increasing the company's ownership percentage of SCL to 74.8% as of December 31, 2025."

Outlook

  • Management expects further EBITDA growth as revenue grows, with Dumont stating, "We expect growth in EBITDA as revenue to grow. We will use our scale and product advantages, together with targeted incentives, to better address every market segment."
  • The company plans to "continue to utilize the company's share repurchase program to increase returns to shareholders."
  • Goldstein noted ongoing opportunity: "There has never been a building, to my knowledge, that delivered these types of results. ... The question is how much further can we go in the next 2 years."

Financial Results

  • Marina Bay Sands reported $806 million in EBITDA with a margin of 50.3%. Dumont noted, "If we had held as expected in our rolling program, our EBITDA would have been lower by $45 million."
  • Macao delivered $608 million in EBITDA for the quarter. Dumont clarified, "Margin at The Venetian was 32.3%, while margin at the Londoner was 28.8%."
  • The company repurchased $500 million in LVS shares and $66 million in SCL shares, increasing SCL ownership to 74.8%.

Q&A

  • Daniel Politzer, JPMorgan: Asked about drivers of VIP rolling chip volume acceleration in Singapore and sustainability. Goldstein responded, "The property is extraordinary, the offerings are great, and we have a lot of fantastic customers in Asia... Nothing really different, just more of the same. More people showing up with -- they got lots of money to gamble, lots of appetite."
  • Politzer: On Macao margin pressure and promotional strategy. Kwan Chum, President and CEO, explained, "We have more rolling business as a proportion of our total gaming. And within non-rolling, it's dominated by the super high end on the premium mass. ... OpEx was higher, yes. We invested more on event costs and we had higher payroll."
  • Elizabeth Dove, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about the path back to $2.7 billion-$2.8 billion EBITDA for Macao. Dumont said, "We're sort of working through some of the changes that we've made. And I think the trajectory is heading in the right direction."
  • Raymond Bowers, Wells Fargo: Asked about the promotional environment in Macao. Chum noted, "The promotional environment remains intense and especially in the premium segments, which is really driving the growth in the market. ... we are at a more stable level now in the current quarter."
  • George Choi, Citigroup: Queried about Marina Bay Sands' higher gaming tax rate and CapEx. Dumont confirmed, "We hit the higher tax rate in July. And in the fourth quarter, there was about $44 million of impact."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts frequently congratulated Goldstein, but focused questions on sustainability of growth, competitive pressures, and margin outlook, indicating a neutral to slightly cautious tone, especially regarding Macao's ongoing challenges.
  • Management expressed confidence in Singapore's trajectory and stability, while adopting a more measured tone around Macao, with Dumont acknowledging, "this quarter may not have produced the results that we want on an EBITDA basis, we see growth, we see better market positioning, we see revenue share growth, but we're heading in the right direction."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence in Singapore remains high, but there is a slightly more defensive tone on Macao margins and promotional intensity.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Singapore’s Marina Bay Sands improved EBITDA from $743 million in Q3 to $806 million in Q4, while Macao’s EBITDA increased modestly from $601 million to $608 million.
  • Macao EBITDA margin declined from 31.5% in Q3 to 28.9% in Q4, with management attributing the drop to segment mix and higher operating expenses.
  • Both quarters emphasized share repurchases and capital returns, but Q4 included a specific increase in SCL ownership.
  • Strategic focus in Q4 shifted toward margin recovery and stabilizing promotional intensity in Macao, while the Singapore outlook remains optimistic.
  • Analyst tone remained consistent, with ongoing questions about Macao recovery and sustainability in Singapore.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited intense promotional competition in Macao, especially in the premium segments.
  • Operating expenses rose due to higher event costs and increased payroll, particularly to support expanded table hours and large-scale events such as the NBA partnership.
  • Margin compression in Macao was attributed to a shift toward lower-margin rolling play and stagnant base mass segment performance.
  • Chum noted, "the market changes day to day, minute by minute. So we will have to observe how competitive dynamics evolve in 2026."

Final Takeaway

Las Vegas Sands highlighted record-setting results at Marina Bay Sands, signaling confidence in sustained growth and high-value tourism in Singapore. In Macao, while EBITDA improved slightly, margin pressure and competitive intensity in the premium segments remain key challenges, with management committed to refining strategy and aiming for margin recovery in 2026. The company reinforced its commitment to shareholder returns through continued share repurchases and maintained a focus on long-term asset investment and operational excellence across its portfolio.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: McEwen Mining to acquire Golden Lake Exploration in all-share deal
data.description:
• McEwen Mining (MUX) and Golden Lake Exploration (GOLXF) have entered into a definitive agreement under which McEwen will acquire all issued and outstanding shares of Golden Lake through a court-approved plan of arrangement, consolidating Golden Lake’s assets into McEwen’s growing portfolio.
  • If the proposed transaction is completed, Golden Lake would become a wholly owned subsidiary of McEwen.
  • Under the proposed transaction, Golden Lake shareholders would receive 0.003876 McEwen shares for each Golden Lake share.
  • The exchange ratio represents an implied offer price of C$0.12 per Golden Lake share, being a premium of 60% to the 20-day volume-weighted average price of the Golden Lake shares as at market close on January 26, 2026.
  • Golden Lake’s principal asset is its 100%-owned Jewel Ridge and Jewel Ridge West projects located adjacent to McEwen’s (MUX) Windfall and Lookout Mountain discoveries, part of the Gold Bar Mine Complex, in the Eureka Mining District of Nevada.

data.title: Samsung Electronics GAAP EPS of KRW 2909.00, revenue of KRW 93.8B
data.description:
• Samsung Electronics press release (SSNLF): Q4 GAAP EPS of KRW 2,909.00.
  • Revenue of KRW 93.8T (+23.7% Y/Y).
  • Operating profit: 20.1 trillion won.

  • “Looking ahead to Q1 2026, the DS Division expects AI and server demand to continue increasing, leading to more opportunities for structural growth,” the company said.

    “In response, the Division will continue to focus on profitability via a strong emphasis on high-performance products,” it added.
